title: Hymenasplenium ikenoi
intro: Hymenasplenium ikenoi, also known as Iken's spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and may also aid in wound healing and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hymenasplenium ikenoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Hymenasplenium ikenoi is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Hymenasplenium ikenoi is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a family of filmy ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. Hymenasplenium ikenoi is further classified into the genus Hymenasplenium, which is a genus of filmy ferns native to Asia. Within the genus, Hymenasplenium ikenoi is one of several species that are characterized by their delicate, lacy fronds. The species is often found growing on trees or rocks in shaded, humid environments.
